title: The Origins of Jazz
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text: Jazz music (0) in the southern United States in the late 19th and early 20th centuries. This unique art form (1) out of a blend of African rhythms, blues, and European harmonies. New Orleans is widely (2) as the birthplace of jazz, where musicians from diverse backgrounds came together to create something entirely new. Early jazz was characterised by improvisation, which (3) it apart from other musical genres of the time. As jazz (4) across America, it evolved into various styles, including swing and bebop. Many talented artists (5) to fame during the jazz age, becoming cultural icons. The music also (6) a part in the civil rights movement, giving African Americans a powerful voice. Today, jazz continues to influence contemporary music and (7) to audiences around the world. Music schools have (8) up the task of preserving this rich heritage while encouraging new generations to explore its possibilities.

example.explanation: 'Originated in' es la expresión formal para indicar dónde algo tuvo su origen.

questions.explanation: 'Grew out of' indica que algo se desarrolló a partir de otra cosa.
'Recognised as' es la expresión correcta para indicar reconocimiento general.
'Set apart' significa distinguir o diferenciar algo de lo demás.
'Spread across' indica difundirse por un área geográfica.
'Rose to fame' es la expresión fija para alcanzar la fama.
'Played a part in' significa tener un papel o participar en algo.
'Appeals to' indica resultar atractivo para alguien.
'Taken up' significa asumir o emprender una tarea o responsabilidad.
